pageDescription: Information for users getting started with the platform either as a user or a plugin developer.
keywords: Getting started, CodeFolio documentation, developer docs, user guides

pageDescription: Getting started with CodeFolio as a user.
content: [{"id":"0cad00a1-dc01-4115-8e8c-c9ffe58ae75c","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Welcome to the user guides section. This area is designed to help you get set up and familiar with how CodeFolio works, from the initial installation to building and managing your site.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"3efc1893-5cde-499d-a17a-719b076a0f67","component":"Text","data":{"content":"To begin, select one of the guides listed in the sidebar on the left. Each page walks through a specific part of the process step by step, so you can follow along and get your project running smoothly.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]}]
keywords: Getting started, CodeFolio documentation, developer docs, user guides

pageDescription: Getting started with CodeFolio as a developer.
keywords: Getting started, CodeFolio documentation, developer docs

pageDescription: Instructions on how to setup CodeFolio on your device locally.
content:
[{"id":"7d89ec80-6358-4096-a0f5-b73e3a6a49f1","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Setting up CodeFolio is straightforward. You will need Node, Git, and a basic familiarity with using a terminal for the initial setup. Once the installation is complete, everything else can be managed through the UI. Before starting the installation, please make sure these requirements are installed on your system.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"ba8ca9ff-64f0-4dea-8060-773779bf520f","component":"CodeBlock","data":{"code":"git clone "https://github.com/hudson1998x/Codefolio"

cd Codefolio

npm run codefolio:factoryreset","language":"shell","filename":"install","showCopy":"true","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"5e8c2cef-58f0-4768-8e45-77d3989fdbb9","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"5a24fee2-e838-4e22-ba4e-cd3037ecfffd","component":"Text","data":{"content":"This will remove the existing content from the CodeFolio repository that is included to demonstrate its documentation. Once removed, your repository will be clear and ready for you to begin building your website, whether you plan to create a portfolio, write documentation, or publish a simple static blog. This also re-initialises your repository, so you can add a remote git service, and your changes are then tracked to your repository, not trying to push content to the CodeFolio repository","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"b67912d7-4069-4961-9c8c-bf71740f5660","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Next we need to run the server so you can manage your website. We do this by using another node command.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"b88db2f5-998c-4356-aaf3-5e4ab0fe3b37","component":"CodeBlock","data":{"code":"npm start","language":"bash","filename":"Run Local Server","showCopy":"true","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"507d12cc-fd08-4d09-b812-8da2da11898a","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"06be307c-98dd-496a-979a-f21784fa481a","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Running this will start up the local web server, this is where you can manage content like pages etc... ","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"49a45d2a-c76b-41fe-9e43-a52fb75f92ce","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Then you can access your admin panel. This is available locally, so there is no need to create an account or go through additional authentication steps. The link below will take you directly to your admin dashboard.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"f8aabec5-4423-4249-8369-95ce845fe45c","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Because CodeFolio works directly with your local Git repository, it does not require a traditional authentication system. Any changes made through the admin panel are simply committed to your repository, so access control is effectively handled by the security of the machine or server where the project is running. In most cases this environment is local or otherwise limited in scope, which keeps the setup simple while still maintaining practical security.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"ec59af6b-ed1f-4ace-9958-6462f55827f1","component":"Text","data":{"content":"http://localhost:3000/en-admin/ ","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"e427a7bd-af93-4312-9604-e69fb892dbb8","component":"Text","data":{"content":"The link above will take you straight to your admin dashboard, once you see the dashboard; your website is ready.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]}]

pageDescription: The next step in setting up CodeFolio is setting up your homepage.
content: [{"id":"8cbd7fab-42e6-4f37-95ba-fa2577613e6e","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Congratulations on getting CodeFolio set up on your device. The installation process has been designed to be as straightforward as possible.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"8f16f9b3-42fe-4f52-8467-eead0c4cac9d","component":"Text","data":{"content":"At this point, you may be looking at your homepage and noticing that the title reads “Loading... | My Portfolio” and that the page itself is still empty. This is expected, as no content has been created yet.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"2af0c401-a124-4576-b2cb-e942c2ba0107","component":"Text","data":{"content":"To get started, you will need to create your first page. Open the admin panel, navigate to Pages in the sidebar, and select + New Entry to begin creating content for your site.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"e3d84a59-8cae-4353-a224-ceffe9b9e687","component":"CodeBlock","data":{"code":"http://localhost:3000/en-admin/","language":"html","filename":"Remember: Admin Panel URL","showCopy":"true","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"38ea70da-a41c-42bc-8fd1-a147f0bd6f70","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"ad07d123-5b06-484b-b869-8c3d74c76cf4","component":"Text","data":{"content":"You'll be greeted by a simple form:","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"f7b61460-9bea-4511-80e0-27c5d1bf9d34","component":"Image","data":{"src":"/media/images/documentation/user-getting-started/1772806044634-pasted-image-1772806043566.png","alt":"Placeholder Image","fit":"cover","lazy":true,"aspectRatio":"auto","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"46d5c531-8d7e-473d-9997-fa88c2818841","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"9bcd9722-aaab-4506-9f30-b2eb5f10c5df","component":"Text","data":{"content":"At the moment, CodeFolio is undergoing major improvements, some parts of the platform aren't as visually appealing yet, but they're functional.  So, as the field explains, we need a page title, and a description. Underneath you can see a button saying visual editor, this is where you bring your pages to life. ","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"26aadd77-9afd-471f-b242-18eb633b6aa4","component":"Image","data":{"src":"/media/images/features/1772754402649-pasted-image-1772754401006.png","alt":"Placeholder Image","fit":"cover","lazy":true,"aspectRatio":"auto","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"ca6469e4-3206-48de-9d6a-0b2c90c2a514","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"be6daeef-7ea5-46d7-8511-910b1fc6caac","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Here you get a hierarchical view, you can search components, drag and drop them into place, and edit their properties, you also see a live preview while you modify the page, so you get a good idea as to what will be on the page, when your finished. ","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"0cec7add-7b00-4cc6-a5d6-8bfe306d56cd","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Once you're happy with the page, and ready to save it, click Finish & Sync, then \"Create Page\", then head over to your homepage (either by clicking the <- Back link in the header, or just going to it in the URL), you'll see your changes have appeared.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]}]
keywords: Getting started, CodeFolio documentation, developer docs, user guides

pageDescription: This guide will show you how to effectively work with prefabs.
content: [{"id":"99651e74-c730-4ed4-85c9-951b5c52b42d","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Prefabs let you create reusable components that can be quickly added again later. They’re especially useful for more complex elements where only a few details need to change each time.  You can use prefabs for things like layouts, UI sections, or other repeatable structures. Prefabs can also be nested inside other prefabs, making it easy to build more advanced designs while keeping everything organized and consistent.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"fd92ef25-03de-4409-b982-b217a762fbfd","component":"Image","data":{"src":"/media/images/documentation/prefab-guide/1772888330292-pasted-image-1772888329657.png","alt":"Placeholder Image","fit":"cover","lazy":true,"aspectRatio":"auto","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"9791fa34-ac0c-4930-86c1-335085b01d82","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"3488b559-2897-4af1-89b6-6bd382643855","component":"Text","data":{"content":"The example above is a component used on the Codefolio homepage. It uses a two-column grid to create a split layout. The first column contains an image, while the second column includes a heading and a short paragraph.  The Prefab Editor works exactly the same as the Page Editor. Once you’re familiar with one, you’ll already know how to use the other.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"8cf73ea4-52bb-45eb-a38e-96b81c86e7cb","component":"Image","data":{"src":"/media/images/documentation/prefab-guide/1772888533609-pasted-image-1772888532779.png","alt":"Placeholder Image","fit":"cover","lazy":true,"aspectRatio":"auto","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"847ec052-f445-4858-a795-8ce62764c499","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"f64da1cc-c063-4180-bb37-f9921d72cb60","component":"Text","data":{"content":"That prefab is then reused three times on the page. There’s also an alternative version that flips the layout, which is used here as well. As you can see, the component is only built once but can be reused multiple times wherever it’s needed.  When you click the “Visual Editor” button under Template Designer, it may look like the page has been cleared. Don’t worry, your component is still there. The editor simply isolates the prefab so you can focus on editing that single component without the visual noise from the rest of the page.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]}]

pageDescription: This guide will show you how to add media to your website.
content: [{"id":"29064257-8914-41b5-bc1d-9b5d222526cc","component":"Alert","data":{"variant":"tip","title":"Known bugs","message":"The area that this documentation is talking about has a few known bugs and will be ironed out in future releases.","showIcon":"true","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"106f8b58-7e41-4776-b1fd-7543ed7f6c06","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"87333c1f-1b53-4203-826e-10c131ae068e","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Adding media to your website is a fairly straightforward process, in your admin panel, in the header, look for \"Media\"","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"07aaf721-1768-48f0-821a-abf2e787f1b6","component":"Image","data":{"src":"/media/images/documentation/adding-media/1772889289373-pasted-image-1772889288588.png","alt":"Placeholder Image","fit":"cover","lazy":true,"aspectRatio":"auto","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"19b66514-1335-409f-9f06-bb8d895df026","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"5ba349ac-f130-4b8b-a247-7646b99a17af","component":"Text","data":{"content":"You'll then be greeted by this UI, the left hand side of this page is the hierarchical view, this shows the directory structure of your \"media\" directory in the repository, You can click on directories to go into them, or click on an image etc... for a preview, you can also right click to get a menu as seen below, this allows you to perform certain tasks on your uploaded files. ","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"6dc844ad-42c6-45ee-aa0d-8101dacc08de","component":"Image","data":{"src":"/media/images/documentation/adding-media/1772889382960-pasted-image-1772889382165.png","alt":"Placeholder Image","fit":"cover","lazy":true,"aspectRatio":"auto","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"a0324bc8-bc8a-407d-bbc3-9365e2f4cc81","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"5802ebdb-d029-487c-972f-e8a837fc5616","component":"Text","data":{"content":"The uploader works for any file type, it also works for the clipboard, so if you've just performed a screenshot, you can paste it straight in as seen below.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"7e28e596-a395-4afb-b9be-3d8112bfaff5","component":"Image","data":{"src":"/media/images/documentation/adding-media/1772889685984-pasted-image-1772889685055.png","alt":"Placeholder Image","fit":"cover","lazy":true,"aspectRatio":"auto","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"f5e938d5-664a-4aef-bab2-2cc26322c6ca","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"64694245-3608-49a0-9bb8-ebfad33404dd","component":"Text","data":{"content":"This can then be used in any of the editors just by adding for example an image:","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"219f1979-ec82-4fb8-b41a-c9fff9e8f7f1","component":"Image","data":{"src":"/media/images/documentation/adding-media/1772889804332-pasted-image-1772889803337.png","alt":"Placeholder Image","fit":"cover","lazy":true,"aspectRatio":"auto","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"7b704b40-8818-4ecd-bbaa-0b922d5ffb01","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"4919cafe-5a60-44ae-9af8-f7f7e9a1e4cb","component":"Text","data":{"content":"And allows you to use your recently uploaded media straight onto a page as expected in any other editor. You can also preview images in the media manager, simply double click on an image, and a preview modal will pop up, giving you a preview of whatever file you've selected. ","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"9e2948e1-9e2e-4833-835f-a2645d67360a","component":"Image","data":{"src":"/media/images/documentation/adding-media/1772889434251-pasted-image-1772889433330.png","alt":"Placeholder Image","fit":"cover","lazy":true,"aspectRatio":"auto","className":""},"children":[]}]
keywords: Media, Images, Video, Codefolio prefabs, help, guide

pageDescription: This guide will show you how to publish your website.
content: [{"id":"66cbbb26-6d61-4b73-a1d0-6051c3fcce21","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Please choose documentation below based off where you plan on deploying the website.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]}]

pageDescription: This guide will show you how to publish your website on github pages.
content: [{"id":"e3118381-a22e-4024-b762-9840430d150f","component":"Text","data":{"content":"CodeFolio was built with GitHub Pages in mind. Once you have added a remote to your repository, you can go ahead and push your project to it.  Pro tip: The Deploy button in the header bar is a quick way to do this if you're not fully comfortable managing staged changes in Git yourself.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"126a5065-2a3c-40ad-b43b-046b22871701","component":"Image","data":{"src":"/media/images/documentation/publishing-your-website/1772891383709-pasted-image-1772891383076.png","alt":"Placeholder Image","fit":"cover","lazy":true,"aspectRatio":"auto","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"ae737ec2-74fd-400e-abac-299d560ec566","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"393cb6e0-b1de-40d9-811a-5e34380be6f4","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Once your repository is up to date, you just need to perform a one-time step to enable GitHub Pages for it. The setup process is very simple and only takes a moment to complete.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"de33d126-6f4b-462e-a072-04f2aa82a6f6","component":"Image","data":{"src":"/media/images/documentation/publishing-your-website/1772891508374-pasted-image-1772891507735.png","alt":"Placeholder Image","fit":"cover","lazy":true,"aspectRatio":"auto","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"51f8a854-8b26-4bd7-b421-fd9f7434ec4e","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"9cf20961-3fca-46b7-8c91-0f21c4a5d95c","component":"Text","data":{"content":"This is how your repository setup should look. Click Pages, then choose the branch you want to deploy from. In most cases this will be main or master, depending on your preference. After selecting the branch, click Save.  Within a few moments your website will be published.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"08f39524-411a-43b7-9652-57c07253bdb6","component":"Text","data":{"content":"GitHub does not always provide a direct link to your site, but the URL follows a standard format:","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"fbc4b849-3746-41e7-8d52-e83a07ad7428","component":"CodeBlock","data":{"code":"https://{githubUsername}.github.io/{repositoryName}","language":"html","filename":"Your website URL:","showCopy":"true","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"12c629fe-8aa2-4bb2-ac1a-b826185eddb3","component":"Divider","data":{"variant":"spacer","size":"md","label":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"cb29ca51-69cc-401a-a94e-ba3e64b9247f","component":"Text","data":{"content":"CodeFolio for instance is under the user: hudson1998x, and the repository is called Codefolio, this means the URL for the repository is:","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"fe573bc9-deeb-47ac-b797-3558e439f09f","component":"Anchor","data":{"url":"https://hudson1998x.github.io/Codefolio","text":"https://hudson1998x.github.io/Codefolio","target":"_self","className":""},"children":[]}]
